subject: Fw:
body:
This is exactly what I told her
Thanks
G

----- Original Message -----
From: helen kim <elkim1234@yahoo.com>
To: Gmax
Sent: Fri Mar 28 13:25:22 2008
Subject: Re:

You and I both know this was not the terms of my termination that were agreed upon.
You, acting as the agent for the company, clearly stated that I would receive one month's severance.
It is truly disappointing that this is the route you and JE have chosen to go.
I will have no choice but to look into alternative options of recourse.

----- Original Message ----
From: "gmax1@mindspring.com" <gmax1@mindspring.com>
To: helen kim <elkim1234@yahoo.com>
Cc: "Kahn, Richard" <rkahn@nysgmail.com>
Sent: Friday, March 28, 2008 12:43:21 PM
Subject: Re:

Helen - I'm not sure where the confusion is - here is what I said to you - JE was giving you 1 month notice - this would be divided by you working  2 weeks and receive 2 weeks paid when you would not be coming to work..the times you would come to work would be the times you generally come in to 457 - not having to  come to 65th - let me know what is the confusion..
------Original Message------
From: helen kim
To: gmax1@mindspring.com
Sent: 27 Mar 2008 18:11
Subject: 

Hi Ghislaine,

During our conversation last Tuesday when I was informed of being terminated you told me that I'd be getting one month's severance.  However, I've just spoken with Rich today and he is saying that I'm due only 2 weeks severance.  Can you please straighten this out with Rich?

Helen
